messages.content:
I'll start by loading the execution-trace skill as required before doing anything else.

Now loading the report-analyzer skill:



Analyzing the lab report PDF from the provided URL using the BloomCell clinical framework and generating the structured JSON output:

The report is a **Functional Laboratory Evaluation** containing a comprehensive blood panel. Based on the document analysis using the BloomCell framework, here are the structured results:

Pattern consistent with B-vitamin-driven methylation block — immediate supplementation warranted.",

Recommendation from BloomCell Key Recommendations: initiate methylated B-complex immediately.",

    "group": "Understanding Your Results",

    "text": "Your Vitamin D level is very low at 18.4 ng/mL — the BloomCell optimal range is 50–80 ng/mL, meaning your level is less than half of where it needs to be. Vitamin D is not just a vitamin — it acts like a hormone in your body and controls over 200 biological processes including your immune system, energy, mood, bone strength, and inflammation control. At this level, you may be experiencing fatigue, low mood, frequent illness, muscle aches, and difficulty recovering after exercise. These results are a snapshot in time, not a permanent condition — with targeted supplementation and sunlight exposure, Vitamin D levels can normalise within 8–12 weeks.",
    "group": "Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D at 18.4 ng/mL is 63% below BloomCell functional floor of 50 ng/mL, severity score 4. Low Vitamin D per BloomCell Narrative results.docx is associated with impaired immune surveillance, reduced serotonin synthesis, poor calcium absorption, and increased inflammatory cytokine activity. Patient-facing language modelled on BloomCell Narrative results.docx. The connection to fatigue, mood, and immunity is drawn directly from the Low column in Biomarkers.xlsx for Vitamin D.",

Depleted iron stores directly impair oxygen transport, mitochondrial energy production, and thyroid hormone synthesis, making this the most urgent actionable biomarker requiring immediate intervention.",

  },
  {

    "date": "06/23/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Start iron bisglycinate 25–50mg with 500mg Vitamin C daily (take away from coffee/tea by 2 hours). Add Vitamin D3 5,000 IU + K2 100mcg daily with largest meal. Begin methylated B-complex (methylfolate + methylcobalamin formula) once daily. Increase red meat consumption to 3–4x per week and add dark leafy greens (spinach, kale) daily. Request full iron panel retest (Ferritin, Serum Iron, TIBC, Iron Saturation) and CBC in 6–8 weeks. Avoid calcium supplements within 2 hours of iron dose. Medical follow-up: rule out occult gastrointestinal blood loss given ferritin this low.",
    "group": "Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Ferritin 9.2 ng/mL (BloomCell floor 50 ng/mL, severity score 5) is the highest priority marker. Iron bisglycinate selected per BloomCell Nutrition MVP for superior absorption and GI tolerability. Vitamin C co-administration enhances non-haem iron absorption per BloomCell Nutrition strategies. Vitamin D3 + K2 combination selected per BloomCell Recovery MVP to address concurrent deficiency and prevent hypercalcaemia risk. Methylated B-complex addresses homocysteine elevation simultaneously. GI referral warranted per BloomCell Key Recommendations when ferritin is below 15 ng/mL to exclude bleeding source.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Ferritin/Iron rows | BloomCell Recovery MVP v2.xlsx - Recovery Strategies sheet - Iron Deficiency section | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Ferritin row",

    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "hsCRP  Result: 3.8  Reference Range: <1.0 mg/L  Flag: HIGH",

This is a persistent pattern requiring a multi-system, 3–6 month intervention strategy addressing root drivers across metabolic, gut, and lifestyle domains.",

    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",

  },
  {
    "id": "a0e8b5c2-1d74-4f39-b6a3-5c9e2f7d0b41",
    "date": "06/23/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Target hsCRP <1.0 mg/L, Ferritin 50–150 ng/mL, Vitamin D 50–80 ng/mL, and Homocysteine <7.0 umol/L by 6-month retest. Retest schedule: 6–8 weeks — iron panel (Ferritin, Serum Iron, TIBC, Iron Saturation) + Vitamin D + hsCRP; 3 months — Homocysteine, B12, Folate, full CBC; 6 months — complete functional panel including thyroid (TSH, Free T3, Free T4), lipid panel, metabolic panel, and inflammatory markers. Supportive testing to consider: GI-MAP (if hsCRP remains elevated at 8 weeks — to identify gut-driven inflammation source), Cyrex Array 3X (if autoimmune markers suspected), DUTCH Plus (if HPA dysregulation contributing to methylation block). Long-term strategies: adopt anti-inflammatory Mediterranean-style diet with omega-3 supplementation (2–3g EPA/DHA daily), establish consistent sleep schedule (7–9 hours), reduce ultra-processed food intake, and implement stress management practices. Reassess training intensity based on ferritin and CRP trajectory — high-intensity training contraindicated until ferritin exceeds 50 ng/mL.",
    "group": "Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Chronic elevated hsCRP (3.8 mg/L vs BloomCell ceiling <1.0) with concurrent iron deficiency, Vitamin D deficiency, and methylation insufficiency represents a multi-system inflammatory and deficiency burden. Per BloomCell diagnostic sequence, this pattern warrants a sustained 3–6 month intervention. Target values derived from BloomCell Biomarkers.xlsx functional ranges. GI-MAP trigger sourced from BloomCell Key supportive Testing sheet for persistently elevated CRP. DUTCH Plus triggered for methylation/HPA co-pattern. Omega-3 and dietary anti-inflammatory strategy sourced from BloomCell Nutrition MVP. Training restriction based on BloomCell Training MVP contraindication rule for severe iron deficiency. Retest schedule modelled on Master Report April 2026 (2).docx Long-Term Strategy section.",
    "referencesource": "Master Report April 2026 (2).docx - Page 5 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - hsCRP/CRP row | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - hsCRP row, Columns Maximum/Severity Score | BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Anti-inflammatory section | BloomCell Training MVP v7.xlsx - Training Strategies sheet - Iron Deficiency section",

Now building the 8 merged records and storing them one by one in Cosmos DB.

**Storing Record 1 of 8 — Internal Notes Highlight:**✅ Record 1 saved. **Storing Record 2 of 8 — Internal Notes Note:**✅ Record 2 saved. **Storing Record 3 of 8 — Understanding Your Results Highlight:**✅ Record 3 saved. **Storing Record 4 of 8 — Understanding Your Results Note:**✅ Record 4 saved. **Storing Record 5 of 8 — Priority Actions Highlight:**✅ Record 5 saved. **Storing Record 6 of 8 — Priority Actions Note:**✅ Record 6 saved. **Storing Record 7 of 8 — Long-Term Strategy Highlight:**✅ Record 7 saved. **Storing Record 8 of 8 — Long-Term Strategy Note:**

8 records saved successfully.

Now updating the original PatientDocument record:



Document updated successfully.
